title: Real team demo uses Codex CLI and Claude CLI proof logs
summary: The YC/team handoff demo should use a real two agent proof path: run Codex CLI against a temp repo to recall Kage memory, run Claude CLI against the same repo to recall the same packet, then render a wide demo video from
body:
The YC/team handoff demo should use a real two-agent proof path: run Codex CLI against a temp repo to recall Kage memory, run Claude CLI against the same repo to recall the same packet, then render a wide demo video from those real proof logs plus Kage recall/code-graph/metrics. Native macOS screencapture failed in this environment, so the shippable artifact is a deterministic Puppeteer/ffmpeg render backed by actual CLI transcripts rather than a GUI screen recording.
Evidence: Ran Codex CLI via script into /tmp/kage-codex-recall-proof.log; it attempted kage_context, fell back to kage CLI, recalled the Payment webhook retry validation gotcha, and answered with src/payments.ts. Ran Claude CLI via script into /tmp/kage-claude-recall-proof.log; it recalled the same packet and source path. Rendered docs/assets/kage-real-team-demo.mp4 and poster with scripts/render-real-team-demo.mjs; ffprobe verified 1600x900, 73.25s, 586 frames.
Verified by: Codex CLI proof log, Claude CLI proof log, kage recall/code-graph/metrics, ffprobe
